data.body:
[G4 container review — parent 4fcd, child a36b `worm-folly-barge` | sibling cross-check: 22bf, 5a6c] One finding (mode: scope overlap). The other 6 cross-child modes read clean (details below).

FINDING — Scope overlap (this child a36b ⟷ sibling 22bf `jerky-vista-amok`)
- finding: a36b's prescriptive ACs re-specify the SAME scoped-freshness remedy that sibling story 22bf already owns and explicitly claims to fix. Two independently-open sibling tickets carry overlapping ACs over the identical file + behavior, with ambiguous ownership of the implementation and its tests.
- criteria: parent AC1 ("a mechanism detects material drift in the code the review was grounded against — e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files"); parent AC3 ("tested: code drift after a signed review → attestation no longer valid").
- location: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` `claim_gate_check` step-3 (whole-HEAD exact-match staleness check); this is both the subject of bug a36b and the line 22bf replaces.
- evidence:
  - a36b ACs prescribe the remedy: AC1 "Claim freshness is scoped to the code the review was grounded against (resolved edit-set / cited files) rather than the whole HEAD sha, so an unrelated commit does NOT stale the attestation"; AC2 "A relevant change (to the cited/edit-set files) STILL invalidates"; test AC "review at X, commit an unrelated file, claim succeeds; review at X, commit a cited file, claim is blocked."
  - Sibling 22bf names the SAME fix and target: description "Content-addressing makes this … immune to … shared-checkout HEAD moves — the specific `worm-folly-barge` complaint"; AC2 "Claim SUCCEEDS when an unrelated file changed after signing (fixes `worm-folly-barge`); claim is BLOCKED when a dependency file's content changed"; test AC "review at X, commit an unrelated file → claim succeeds; commit a dependency file → claim blocked."
  - Both target the identical `claim_gate_check` step-3 replacement and carry near-verbatim, overlapping test scenarios (unrelated-file → succeeds; dependency/cited-file → blocked). a36b is currently an independently-open BUG ticket (status: open) with prescriptive implementation ACs, not a folded-in report.
  - The relationship edge is loose: a36b `relates_to` 22bf (not `fixes`/blocking), so neither ticket pins single ownership of the change.
- scenarios:
  (a) Both 22bf and a36b are independently claimed/implemented → two competing edits to `claim_gate_check` step-3 and duplicate/conflicting test suites for the same unrelated-file-succeeds / cited-file-blocked behavior.
  (b) a36b is implemented as a narrower whole-HEAD tweak that diverges from 22bf's content-addressed `{path: content-hash}` design → two inconsistent fixes for one root cause.
- impact: Risk of duplicate or conflicting implementation of one behavior across two open sibling tickets; ambiguous ownership of the `worm-folly-barge` fix and its tests.
- checklist_item: scope overlap.
- suggested_fix: Make exactly one ticket own the scoped-freshness implementation + tests. Either (i) convert a36b into a non-implementing bug/report that 22bf "fixes" (fold its repro + unrelated-file/cited-file scenarios into 22bf's test AC as verification), or (ii) upgrade the a36b→22bf link to an explicit `fixes`/blocking relation and strip a36b's prescriptive implementation ACs, leaving it as the reproduction/acceptance witness. Either way the `claim_gate_check` step-3 change and its scenarios live in one place. (This overlap was also recorded from 22bf's side; noting it here so the duplication is visible from both tickets.)

The other 6 modes (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied):
- Implicit shared state — CLEAN for a36b: the signed-manifest-schema coordination concern lives on the 22bf⟷5a6c axis (both extend `build_manifest`/`sign_plan_review`'s signed payload). a36b is a bug report and prescribes no manifest-schema write of its own. Not flagged.
- Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN: a36b's diagnosis ("the code-binding is too coarse (whole-HEAD) rather than scoped to the review's resolved edit-set / cited files") agrees with the parent and with 22bf/5a6c's dependency basis (`file_impact` ∪ citations). No contradiction.
- Dependency gap — CLEAN: a36b declares `relates_to` 22bf; the fix-relevant edge between the bug and its remedy story is present (its tightness is addressed in the scope-overlap fix above, not a missing-edge gap).
- Ordering violation — CLEAN: a36b is a bug/witness, not a story sequenced ahead of or behind another deliverable; no ordering precedence is created or violated. (The Story 1 → Story 2 ordering is the 22bf → 5a6c axis, correctly carried by 5a6c `depends_on` 22bf.)
- Consumer impact — CLEAN: the sole claim-path consumer (`src/rebar/_commands/claim.py`) reads `ok`/`reason`; the scoped-freshness fix changes the `stale-head` verdict internals only, not the gate's read contract. No dangling consumer introduced by a36b.
- Residual references — CLEAN: a36b introduces no orphaned/duplicate reference; it points at the existing `stale-head` behavior and the `require_plan_review_for_claim` gate, both real.
